{{nihongo|'''Oosakayamaguchi Shrine'''|大坂山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

Shrine|lt=Ohosakanoyamakuchi Shrine|WD=Q135038960}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} [[JA:大坂山口神社 (香芝市逢坂)]] {{wikidata link|Q135069036}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 大坂山口神社 |画像 = [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)2.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|香芝市|Q696643}}{{jalink|逢坂 (香芝市)||逢坂}}5-831 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|大山祇命}}、{{jalink|須佐之男命}}、{{jalink|神大市比売命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月16日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=大坂
山口神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)1.jpg|thumb|250px|鳥居と拝殿]] '''大坂山口神社'''（おおさかやまぐちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|香芝市|Q696643}}{{jalink|逢坂 (香芝市)||逢坂}}（おうさか）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 概要 === {{jalink|近鉄大阪線|Q1052545}}の{{jalink|二上駅|Q7034597}}の北東近隣に鎮座している。 穴虫地区の{{jalink|大坂山口神社 (香芝市穴虫)|Q135069035|大坂山口神社}}と共に、『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』の{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}9年の記述にある「赤盾八枚・赤矛八竿を以て墨坂神（すみさかのかみ）を祠り、黒盾八枚・黒矛八竿を以て大坂神（おおさかのかみ）を祠れ」という夢告における、大坂神を祠った神社に比定されている（ちなみに、墨坂神（すみさかのかみ）は{{jalink|宇陀市|Q863747}}の{{jalink|墨坂神社 (宇陀市)||墨坂神社}}に比定されている）[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf 逢坂・大坂山口神社] - 香芝市二上山博物館[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html 大坂山口神社本殿、神像群及び宝物類(大坂山口神社本殿、大坂山口神社神像及び宝物類)] - 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース。 また、同じく穴虫地区の大坂山口神社と共に、{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427|大和国の式内社}}の1つである大坂山口神社の論社となっている。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|大山祇命}} * {{jalink|須佐之男命}} * {{jalink|神大市比売命}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおさかやまくちしんしや かしはしおうさか}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|香芝市|Q696643}}{{jalink|逢坂 (香芝市)||逢坂}}5-831 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|大山祇命}}、{{jalink|須佐之男命}}、{{jalink|神大市比売命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月16日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=大坂
山口神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)1.jpg|thumb|250px|鳥居と拝殿]] '''大坂山口神社'''（おおさかやまぐちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|香芝市|Q696643}}{{jalink|逢坂 (香芝市)||逢坂}}（おうさか）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 === 概要 === {{jalink|近鉄大阪線|Q1052545}}の{{jalink|二上駅|Q7034597}}の北東近隣に鎮座している。 穴虫地区の{{jalink|大坂山口神社 (香芝市穴虫)|Q135069035|大坂山口神社}}と共に、『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』の{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}9年の記述にある「赤盾八枚・赤矛八竿を以て墨坂神（すみさかのかみ）を祠り、黒盾八枚・黒矛八竿を以て大坂神（おおさかのかみ）を祠れ」という夢告における、大坂神を祠った神社に比定されている（ちなみに、墨坂神（すみさかのかみ）は{{jalink|宇陀市|Q863747}}の{{jalink|墨坂神社 (宇陀市)||墨坂神社}}に比定されている）[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf 逢坂・大坂山口神社] - 香芝市二上山博物館[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html 大坂山口神社本殿、神像群及び宝物類(大坂山口神社本殿、大坂山口神社神像及び宝物類)] - 奈良県歴史文化資源データベース。 また、同じく穴虫地区の大坂山口神社と共に、{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427|大和国の式内社}}の1つである大坂山口神社の論社となっている。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|大山祇命}} * {{jalink|須佐之男命}} * {{jalink|神大市比売命}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおさかやまくちしんしや かしはしおうさか}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

|Name = Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashiba City|Q696643}}{{jalink|Ōsaka (Kashiba City)||Ōsaka}}5-831 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}、{{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{jalink|Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine, former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites etc. = |Annual festival = October 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōsaka
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii gate and worship hall]] '''Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine''' (おおさかやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōsaka (Kashiba City)||Ōsaka}} (Ōsaka), {{jalink|Kashiba City|Q696643}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. === Overview === The shrine is enshrined in the northeastern vicinity of {{jalink|Futakami Station|Q7034597}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545}}. Together with {{jalink|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi, Kashiba City)|Q135069035|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine}} in the Anamushi district, it is identified as the shrine that enshrined the Ōsaka deity mentioned in a dream oracle recorded in the 9th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} in the {{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}} and {{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}: “With eight red shields and eight red halberds, enshrine the god of Sumisaka (Sumisaka-no-kami); with eight black shields and eight black halberds, enshrine the god of Ōsaka (Ōsaka-no-kami).” (Incidentally, the god of Sumisaka is identified with {{jalink|Sumisaka Shrine (Uda City)||Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{jalink|Uda City|Q863747}}.)[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf Ōsaka and Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine] - Kashiba City Futakami Mountain Museum[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items (Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items of Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine)] - Nara Prefecture Historical and Cultural Resources Database. In addition, together with the Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine in the Anamushi district, it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine, one of the shrines listed in the {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaka Yamaguchi Shrine Kashiba Ōsaka}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

|Name = Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashiba City|Q696643}}{{jalink|Ōsaka (Kashiba City)||Ōsaka}}5-831 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}、{{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{jalink|Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine, former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites etc. = |Annual festival = October 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōsaka
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii gate and worship hall]] '''Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine''' (おおさかやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōsaka (Kashiba City)||Ōsaka}} (Ōsaka), {{jalink|Kashiba City|Q696643}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. === Overview === The shrine is enshrined in the northeastern vicinity of {{jalink|Futakami Station|Q7034597}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545}}. Together with {{jalink|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi, Kashiba City)|Q135069035|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine}} in the Anamushi district, it is identified as the shrine that enshrined the Ōsaka deity mentioned in a dream oracle recorded in the 9th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} in the {{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}} and {{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}: “With eight red shields and eight red halberds, enshrine the god of Sumisaka (Sumisaka-no-kami); with eight black shields and eight black halberds, enshrine the god of Ōsaka (Ōsaka-no-kami).” (Incidentally, the god of Sumisaka is identified with {{jalink|Sumisaka Shrine (Uda City)||Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{jalink|Uda City|Q863747}}.)[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf Ōsaka and Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine] - Kashiba City Futakami Mountain Museum[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items (Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items of Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine)] - Nara Prefecture Historical and Cultural Resources Database. In addition, together with the Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine in the Anamushi district, it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine, one of the shrines listed in the {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaka Yamaguchi Shrine Kashiba Ōsaka}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

|Name = Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashiba City|Q696643}}{{jalink|Ōsaka (Kashiba City)||Ōsaka}}5-831 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}、{{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{jalink|Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine, former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites etc. = |Annual festival = October 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōsaka
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii gate and worship hall]] '''Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine''' (おおさかやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōsaka (Kashiba City)||Ōsaka}} (Ōsaka), {{jalink|Kashiba City|Q696643}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. === Overview === The shrine is enshrined in the northeastern vicinity of {{jalink|Futakami Station|Q7034597}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545}}. Together with {{jalink|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi, Kashiba City)|Q135069035|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine}} in the Anamushi district, it is identified as the shrine that enshrined the Ōsaka deity mentioned in a dream oracle recorded in the 9th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} in the {{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}} and {{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}: “With eight red shields and eight red halberds, enshrine the god of Sumisaka (Sumisaka-no-kami); with eight black shields and eight black halberds, enshrine the god of Ōsaka (Ōsaka-no-kami).” (Incidentally, the god of Sumisaka is identified with {{jalink|Sumisaka Shrine (Uda City)||Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{jalink|Uda City|Q863747}}.)[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf Ōsaka and Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine] - Kashiba City Futakami Mountain Museum[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items (Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items of Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine)] - Nara Prefecture Historical and Cultural Resources Database. In addition, together with the Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine in the Anamushi district, it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine, one of the shrines listed in the {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaka Yamaguchi Shrine Kashiba Ōsaka}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]]

Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii gate and worship hall]] '''Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine''' (おおさかやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōsaka (Kashiba City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsaka}} (Ōsaka), {{ill|lt=Kashiba City|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. === Overview === The shrine is enshrined in the northeastern vicinity of {{ill|lt=Futakami Station|Q7034597|WD=Q7034597|1=Nijō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|1=Osaka Line}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi, Kashiba City)|Q135069035|WD=Q135069035|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine|1=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi)}} in the Anamushi district, it is identified as the shrine that enshrined the Ōsaka deity mentioned in a dream oracle recorded in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}: “With eight red shields and eight red halberds, enshrine the god of Sumisaka (Sumisaka-no-kami); with eight black shields and eight black halberds, enshrine the god of Ōsaka (Ōsaka-no-kami).” (Incidentally, the god of Sumisaka is identified with {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda City|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}.)[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf Ōsaka and Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine] - Kashiba City Futakami Mountain Museum[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items (Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items of Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine)] - Nara Prefecture Historical and Cultural Resources Database. In addition, together with the Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine in the Anamushi district, it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine, one of the shrines listed in the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaka Yamaguchi Shrine Kashiba Ōsaka}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Together with {{ill|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi, Kashiba City)|Q135069035|WD=Q135069035|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine|1=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi)}} in the Anamushi district, it is identified as the shrine that enshrined the Ōsaka deity mentioned in a dream oracle recorded in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}: “With eight red shields and eight red halberds, enshrine the god of Sumisaka (Sumisaka-no-kami); with eight black shields and eight black halberds, enshrine the god of Ōsaka (Ōsaka-no-kami).” (Incidentally, the god of Sumisaka is identified with {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda City|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}.)[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf Ōsaka and Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine] - Kashiba City Futakami Mountain Museum[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items (Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items of Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine)] - Nara Prefecture Historical and Cultural Resources Database. In addition, together with the Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine in the Anamushi district, it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine, one of the shrines listed in the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii gate and worship hall]] '''Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine''' (おおさかやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōsaka (Kashiba City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsaka}} (Ōsaka), {{ill|lt=Kashiba City|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. === Overview === The shrine is enshrined in the northeastern vicinity of {{ill|lt=Futakami Station|Q7034597|WD=Q7034597|1=Nijō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|1=Osaka Line}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi, Kashiba City)|Q135069035|WD=Q135069035|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine|1=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi)}} in the Anamushi district, it is identified as the shrine that enshrined the Ōsaka deity mentioned in a dream oracle recorded in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}: “With eight red shields and eight red halberds, enshrine the god of Sumisaka (Sumisaka-no-kami); with eight black shields and eight black halberds, enshrine the god of Ōsaka (Ōsaka-no-kami).” (Incidentally, the god of Sumisaka is identified with {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda City|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}.)[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf Ōsaka and Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine] - Kashiba City Futakami Mountain Museum[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items (Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items of Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine)] - Nara Prefecture Historical and Cultural Resources Database. In addition, together with the Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine in the Anamushi district, it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine, one of the shrines listed in the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii gate and worship hall]] '''Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine''' (おおさかやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōsaka (Kashiba City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsaka}} (Ōsaka), {{ill|lt=Kashiba City|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. === Overview === The shrine is enshrined in the northeastern vicinity of {{ill|lt=Futakami Station|Q7034597|WD=Q7034597|1=Nijō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|1=Osaka Line}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi, Kashiba City)|Q135069035|WD=Q135069035|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine|1=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi)}} in the Anamushi district, it is identified as the shrine that enshrined the Ōsaka deity mentioned in a dream oracle recorded in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}: “With eight red shields and eight red halberds, enshrine the god of Sumisaka (Sumisaka-no-kami); with eight black shields and eight black halberds, enshrine the god of Ōsaka (Ōsaka-no-kami).” (Incidentally, the god of Sumisaka is identified with {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda City|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}.)[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf Ōsaka and Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine] - Kashiba City Futakami Mountain Museum[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items (Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items of Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine)] - Nara Prefecture Historical and Cultural Resources Database. In addition, together with the Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine in the Anamushi district, it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine, one of the shrines listed in the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

[[JA:大坂山口神社 (香芝市逢坂)]] {{wikidata link|Q135069036}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashiba City|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Ōsaka (Kashiba City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsaka}}5-831 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}、{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites etc. = |Annual festival = October 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōsaka
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii gate and worship hall]] '''Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine''' (おおさかやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōsaka (Kashiba City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsaka}} (Ōsaka), {{ill|lt=Kashiba City|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. === Overview === The shrine is enshrined in the northeastern vicinity of {{ill|lt=Futakami Station|Q7034597|WD=Q7034597|1=Nijō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|1=Osaka Line}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi, Kashiba City)|Q135069035|WD=Q135069035|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine|1=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi)}} in the Anamushi district, it is identified as the shrine that enshrined the Ōsaka deity mentioned in a dream oracle recorded in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}: “With eight red shields and eight red halberds, enshrine the god of Sumisaka (Sumisaka-no-kami); with eight black shields and eight black halberds, enshrine the god of Ōsaka (Ōsaka-no-kami).” (Incidentally, the god of Sumisaka is identified with {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda City|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}.)[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf Ōsaka and Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine] - Kashiba City Futakami Mountain Museum[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items (Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items of Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine)] - Nara Prefecture Historical and Cultural Resources Database. In addition, together with the Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine in the Anamushi district, it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine, one of the shrines listed in the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii gate and worship hall]] '''Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine''' (おおさかやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōsaka (Kashiba City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsaka}} (Ōsaka), {{ill|lt=Kashiba City|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. === Overview === The shrine is enshrined in the northeastern vicinity of {{ill|lt=Futakami Station|Q7034597|WD=Q7034597|1=Nijō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|1=Osaka Line}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi, Kashiba City)|Q135069035|WD=Q135069035|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine|1=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi)}} in the Anamushi district, it is identified as the shrine that enshrined the Ōsaka deity mentioned in a dream oracle recorded in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}: “With eight red shields and eight red halberds, enshrine the god of Sumisaka (Sumisaka-no-kami); with eight black shields and eight black halberds, enshrine the god of Ōsaka (Ōsaka-no-kami).” (Incidentally, the god of Sumisaka is identified with {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda City|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}.)[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf Ōsaka and Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine] - Kashiba City Futakami Mountain Museum[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items (Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items of Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine)] - Nara Prefecture Historical and Cultural Resources Database. In addition, together with the Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine in the Anamushi district, it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine, one of the shrines listed in the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

as (P460) == * {{ill|Ohosakanoyamakuchi Shrine|lt=Ohosakanoyamakuchi Shrine|WD=Q135038960|1=Ohosakanoyamakuchi Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} [[JA:大坂山口神社 (香芝市逢坂)]] {{wikidata link|Q135069036}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)2.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashiba City|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Ōsaka (Kashiba City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsaka}}5-831 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}、{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage sites etc. = |Annual festival = October 16 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōsaka
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[File:Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja (osaka)1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii gate and worship hall]] '''Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine''' (おおさかやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōsaka (Kashiba City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōsaka}} (Ōsaka), {{ill|lt=Kashiba City|Q696643|WD=Q696643|1=Kashiba, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. === Overview === The shrine is enshrined in the northeastern vicinity of {{ill|lt=Futakami Station|Q7034597|WD=Q7034597|1=Nijō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|1=Osaka Line}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi, Kashiba City)|Q135069035|WD=Q135069035|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine|1=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi)}} in the Anamushi district, it is identified as the shrine that enshrined the Ōsaka deity mentioned in a dream oracle recorded in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}: “With eight red shields and eight red halberds, enshrine the god of Sumisaka (Sumisaka-no-kami); with eight black shields and eight black halberds, enshrine the god of Ōsaka (Ōsaka-no-kami).” (Incidentally, the god of Sumisaka is identified with {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda City|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}.)[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf Ōsaka and Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine] - Kashiba City Futakami Mountain Museum[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items (Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items of Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine)] - Nara Prefecture Historical and Cultural Resources Database. In addition, together with the Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine in the Anamushi district, it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine, one of the shrines listed in the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamioichihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Osaka-yamaguchi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Kashiba)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:香芝市の建築物]] [[Category:香芝市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]]

Together with {{ill|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi, Kashiba City)|Q135069035|WD=Q135069035|lt=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine|1=Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine (Anamushi)}} in the Anamushi district, it is identified as the shrine that enshrined the Ōsaka deity mentioned in a dream oracle recorded in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}: “With eight red shields and eight red halberds, enshrine the god of Sumisaka (Sumisaka-no-kami); with eight black shields and eight black halberds, enshrine the god of Ōsaka (Ōsaka-no-kami).” (Incidentally, the god of Sumisaka is identified with {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda City|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}.)[https://www.city.kashiba.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/1655.pdf Ōsaka and Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine] - Kashiba City Futakami Mountain Museum[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00217.html Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items (Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine Main Hall, Sacred Statues and Treasure Items of Ōsaka Yamaguchi Shrine)] - Nara Prefecture Historical and Cultural Resources Database.
